en_body:
Lebanon! Homeland, Earth-and-Sky...through thousands upon ten-thousands of reborn lifetimes, how could one not remain entangled in the green of cedar forests, fragrant with the wax of King Solomon and the Old Testament! Oh, brocaded mountains and rivers! Oh, evergreen forests fragrant with breath, steeped and diffused from the bursting green rays of leaves trembling with sap! Oh, Lebanon!...a human shadow once journeyed there loving. The shadow glided softly to kiss every grain of air in Kahlil Gibran's earth and sky after reading his story "The Broken Wings"...The shadow embraced itself into that place to conceive within itself a body and all the hardship and travail of one without God! Oh, oh, Lebanon!
 Lebanon! The pale milk-moon newly fourteen bent down to bite between all its teeth and tongue a young blade of malt, tart-sweet with the budding of a maiden! Oh, homeland...a road was drawn to divide in two and travel forever; a road tearing open the body of Mother, Ancestors, Fathers!
 ...Will ever? Will ever? Oh, will ever...that road cease its flowing?!

Lebanon
 Lebanon! Homeland, Earth-and-Sky...through thousands upon ten-thousands of reborn lifetimes, how could one not remain entangled in the green of cedar forests, fragrant with the wax of King Solomon and the Old Testament! Oh, brocaded mountains and rivers! Oh, evergreen forests fragrant with breath, steeped and diffused from the bursting green rays of leaves trembling with sap! Oh, Lebanon!...a human shadow once journeyed there loving. The shadow glided softly to kiss every grain of air in Kahlil Gibran's earth and sky after reading his story "The Broken Wings"...The shadow embraced itself into that place to conceive within itself a body and all the hardship and travail of one without God! Oh, oh, Lebanon!
 Lebanon! The pale milk-moon newly fourteen bent down to bite between all its teeth and tongue a young blade of malt, tart-sweet with the budding of a maiden! Oh, homeland...a road was drawn to divide in two and travel forever; a road tearing open the body of Mother, Ancestors, Fathers!
 ...Will ever? Will ever? Oh, will ever...that road cease its flowing?!
note: The cedars evoke Solomon's Temple and the Old Testament, while “The Broken Wings” invokes the Lebanese poet Kahlil Gibran.
